User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the Hosana City Housing Management System, with specific roles and access levels.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The unique email address of the user.

role.description: The assigned role of the user, determining their access and permissions within the system. (Possible values: Mayor, Sub-city Official, Woreda Administrator, Kebele Officer)

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the user.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the user's account is active or inactive.

subCityId.description: Reference to the SubCity this user is associated with, if applicable (e.g., for Sub-city officials). (Relationship: SubCity 1:N User)

woredaId.description: Reference to the Woreda this user is associated with, if applicable (e.g., for Woreda administrators). (Relationship: Woreda 1:N User)

kebeleId.description: Reference to the Kebele this user is associated with, if applicable (e.g., for Kebele officers). (Relationship: Kebele 1:N User)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user account information was updated.

SubCity.title: SubCity
SubCity.description: Represents a sub-city, which is the highest administrative division in the Hosana City administrative hierarchy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubCity entity.

name.description: The unique name of the sub-city.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the sub-city record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the sub-city record was updated.

Woreda.title: Woreda
Woreda.description: Represents a woreda, an administrative division nested within a SubCity in the Hosana City hierarchy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Woreda entity.

subCityId.description: Reference to the SubCity to which this woreda belongs. (Relationship: SubCity 1:N Woreda)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the woreda record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the woreda record was updated.

Kebele.title: Kebele
Kebele.description: Represents a kebele, the lowest administrative division, nested within a Woreda in the Hosana City hierarchy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Kebele entity.

woredaId.description: Reference to the Woreda to which this kebele belongs. (Relationship: Woreda 1:N Kebele)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the kebele record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the kebele record was updated.

House.title: House
House.description: Represents a single housing record, including details about the house, its location, and the householder.

id.description: Unique identifier for the House entity.

houseNumber.description: A unique identifying number assigned to the house within its administrative division.

subCityId.description: Reference to the SubCity where the house is located. (Relationship: SubCity 1:N House)

woredaId.description: Reference to the Woreda where the house is located. (Relationship: Woreda 1:N House)

kebeleId.description: Reference to the Kebele where the house is located. (Relationship: Kebele 1:N House)

householderName.description: The full name of the primary householder or head of the household.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the householder.

nationalId.description: The national ID number of the householder (optional field).

familySize.description: The total number of individuals residing in the household.

houseType.description: The type of ownership or tenure for the house. (Possible values: owned, rented, government)

addressDescription.description: A comprehensive textual description of the house's full address.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the house's location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the house's location.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this housing record. (Relationship: User 1:N House)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the housing record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the housing record was updated.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records all significant user actions (create, update, delete) on data entities for accountability and historical tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

action.description: The type of action performed (e.g., CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE).

tableName.description: The name of the database table or entity affected by the action.

recordId.description: The unique identifier of the specific record within the tableName that was affected.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the action was performed.

description.description: An optional detailed description of the audit event, including changes made.

definition.description: Stores user profiles within the system. Each document includes the user's role and associated administrative IDs (`subCityId`, `woredaId`, `kebeleId`) for DBAC (Database Access Control), enabling role and location-based authorization checks directly from the user's profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, typically provided by Firebase Authentication (request.auth.uid).

definition.description: Represents the top-level administrative divisions. These documents are generally accessible for lookup by relevant users and manageable by higher-level officials (Mayor, Sub-city officials).

params.name: subCityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the SubCity.

definition.description: Represents woredas, administrative divisions nested within a SubCity. Each Woreda document includes a denormalized 'subCityId' field for Authorization Independence, allowing rules to verify its parent SubCity directly without additional 'get()' calls. Accessible for lookup, and manageable by Sub-city officials and Woreda administrators within their scope.

params.name: subCityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent SubCity.

params.name: woredaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Woreda.

definition.description: Represents kebeles, the lowest administrative division, nested within a Woreda. Each Kebele document includes denormalized 'subCityId' and 'woredaId' fields for full Authorization Independence, enabling rules to verify the complete administrative path without 'get()' calls on parent documents. Accessible for lookup, and manageable by Woreda administrators and Kebele officers within their scope.

params.name: subCityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent SubCity.

params.name: woredaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Woreda.

params.name: kebeleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Kebele.

definition.description: The primary collection for all housing records. Each document includes denormalized 'subCityId', 'woredaId', and 'kebeleId' fields. This is CRITICAL for Authorization Independence and QAPs, enabling atomic rule checks for location-based access without requiring 'get()' calls on administrative hierarchy documents. The 'createdBy' field facilitates ownership-based access control.

params.name: houseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the house record.

definition.description: Records all significant user actions (create, update, delete) on data entities for accountability and historical tracking. Access is typically restricted to higher-level officials (Mayor, Sub-city officials) for read-only purposes.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier of the audit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for the Hosana City Housing Management System is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and standardized Access Modeling. This approach minimizes complexity in security rules, enhances debuggability, and ensures scalability.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved primarily through strategic denormalization. For instance:
*   **User Documents (`/users/{userId}`):** The user's `role` and associated administrative IDs (`subCityId`, `woredaId`, `kebeleId`) are stored directly on their user profile document. This enables security rules to fetch the user's authorization context with a single `get()` operation on their own profile (`get(/users/$(request.auth.uid))`) and then perform all subsequent authorization checks against `resource.data` without further hierarchical `get()` calls. This adheres to the DBAC mandate (roles in DB) while still enabling robust authorization checks.
*   **Administrative Hierarchy (`/subCities/{subCityId}/woredas/{woredaId}/kebeles/{kebeleId}`):** Each Woreda document explicitly includes `subCityId`, and each Kebele document explicitly includes both `subCityId` and `woredaId` (denormalizing from its parent Woreda's parent SubCity). This prevents rules from needing to `get()` parent documents to establish the full administrative path, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic for administrative division access.
*   **House Documents (`/houses/{houseId}`):** Crucially, every `House` document includes denormalized `subCityId`, `woredaId`, and `kebeleId`. This allows any rule checking access to a house (e.g., a Kebele officer accessing houses in their kebele) to directly compare `request.auth.kebeleId` (from their user profile) with `resource.data.kebeleId` without requiring any `get()` operations on the administrative hierarchy documents. This is a foundational aspect for secure and performant rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Structural Segregation:**
*   **Homogeneous Security Posture:** Each top-level collection (`/users`, `/subCities`, `/houses`, `/auditLogs`) or sub-collection (`/subCities/{subCityId}/woredas`, `/subCities/{subCityId}/woredas/{woredaId}/kebeles`) is designed to contain documents with uniform security requirements. For example, all documents in `/houses` represent housing records that require location-based access control. This avoids mixing data with different access needs, simplifying rule sets.
*   **Queryable Authorization Fields:** Because `subCityId`, `woredaId`, and `kebeleId` are denormalized into `House` documents, clients can issue queries like `db.collection('houses').where('kebeleId', '==', userKebeleId)`. Firestore security rules can then efficiently validate these queries by ensuring that the `resource.data.kebeleId` matches the `request.auth.kebeleId` (obtained from the user's profile), thus enforcing QAPs by ensuring no unauthorized data is returned or accessed. The structure enables rules to assert *all* retrieved documents satisfy the authorization criteria, rather than attempting to filter results after retrieval.
